sections.title: Weather
sections.summary: Depart only during a clear break for troop meeting arrival and school departure; if rain persists, use an indoor backup or brief covered wait rather than canceling.
details: Source: staged advisory, not a live weather connector.

sections.title: Mission
sections.summary:
What matters now: your calendar is stacked with family commitments starting May 11, plus two pending approvals that need decisions, but the only one that truly requires urgency is the front door unlock because it’s high-risk and needs second factor.  
What can wait: the duplicate vendor-package approvals, the parent-message drafts, and any deeper wealth strategy work—your financial-independence lane is already clear: build one small digital asset or productized offer, not a moonshot.  
Clean next move: clear the approval queue in order of risk, then block one focused session to pick a single repeatable problem and ship a one-page offer with a simple validation link to 10–20 targeted people.
details: First-light today is less about the clock than the loadout: Chris has a short runway before family coordination starts stacking—especially Rebekah’s hair appointment, Anna’s observation, and the mid-May Chris/Stag sequence. The second-order effect is that “meeting prep with discretion” and “voice tightening” should happen early, because once the calendar gets moving, attention gets fragmented and the workshop overbuild risk rises.

sections.title: Formation
sections.summary: Stewardship under pressure — move with restraint, choose the one necessary action, and let the rest wait.
